title: dalbergia xylocarpa
intro: Dalbergia xylocarpa, also known as the Indian rosewood, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to the tropical forests of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Dalbergia xylocarpa include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia xylocarpa include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be used to make medicines and other health supplements.
